item.title: JPMorgan recruits Bank of America services banker in latest senior hire from rival
item.description: JPMorgan Chase has hired a senior investment banker from Bank of America to lead its North America business services investment banking team, the latest in a string of senior recruits it вҖӢhas made from its Wall Street rival.

item.title: Freeport-McMoRan confident in copper demand despite Iran conflict, CEO says
item.description: Freeport-McMoRan expects demand for copper for use in electrification, data centers and other вҖӢhigh-tech areas to remain resilient despite market jitters tied to the Iran conflict, CEO Kathleen Quirk told Reuters вҖҢon Monday.

item.title: Exclusive: American Residential Services explores $3.5 billion-plus sale, sources say
item.description: The private equity owner of American Residential Services (ARS) is exploring a sale of the heating, ventilation, and air вҖӢconditioning (HVAC) and plumbing services provider that could value it at вҖҢover $3.5 billion, according to three sources familiar with the matter.

item.title: Musk's Starlink denied telecoms licence in Namibia
item.description: Namibia has turned down applications from Elon Musk's satellite internet provider Starlink for вҖӢa telecommunications service licence and access вҖҢto radio spectrum, a notice in the Southern African country's government gazette showed.

item.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman exits Helion Energy's board as firms explore partnership
item.description: OpenAI Chief Executive Sam Altman said on Monday he has stepped down from the board of directors of Helion Energy, the fusion startup вҖӢhe has backed since 2015, as the companies start to explore вҖҢworking together "at significant scale".

item.title: Ben & Jerry's Foundation joins lawsuit challenging The Magnum Ice Cream Company
item.description: The Ben & Jerry's Foundation, a non-profit funded by the ice cream brand, said on Monday it had won a court вҖӢruling to join a lawsuit challenging its ultimate parent company, вҖҢThe Magnum Ice Cream Company , over contractual obligations and independence.

item.title: China's AliExpress tells EU lawmakers it is working to comply with law
item.description: Chinese online shopping site AliExpress said on Monday it was improving controls to comply with European Union regulations as European lawmakers grilled executives over вҖӢsales of dangerous and counterfeit products in the bloc.

item.title: Risk-off trade keeps gold volatile as Iran war spooks investors
item.description: Acute volatility вҖӢin gold prices is set to persist in the short term as investors cut risk, вҖҢwith the Iran war boosting inflation fears, curbing bets on interest rate cuts, and weighing on the outlook for global growth, analysts said.

item.title: French Energy Ministry considers increasing oil refining capacity
item.description: The вҖҢFrench Energy Ministry has started evaluating the possibility of increasing the capacity of the country's five вҖӢoil refineries in a bid to вҖӢsoften the impact of the conflict in Iran вҒ on energy prices, it said on вҖӢMonday.

item.title: Madison Dearborn-backed defense contractor AEVEX files for US IPO
item.description: Defense technology prime вҖӢcontractor AEVEX, backed by вҖҢbuyout firm Madison Dearborn Partners, on Monday filed for вҖӢan initial public offering вҖӢin the United States.

item.title: Apple to hold annual developers conference in June
item.description: Apple вҖӢsaid вҖҢon Monday вҖӢit вҖӢwould вҒ host вҖӢits annual вҖӢWorldwide Developers вҖӢConference вҖӢonline from вҖҢJune вҒ 8 to вҖӢ12.

item.title: AI may boost euro area productivity growth by 4% in 10 years, ECB says
item.description: Artificial intelligence could lift euro zone productivity growth by more than 4 percentage points over the next decade, although a вҖӢprolonged energy shock could slow progress, European Central Bank chief вҖҢeconomist Philip Lane said on Monday.

item.title: Gilead nearing up to $2 billion buyout of biotech Ouro Medicines, FT reports
item.description: Gilead вҖӢSciences is nearing an вҖҢacquisition of Ouro Medicines, a biotech focused on immune disorder вҖӢtreatments, for up to $2 вҖӢbillion, the Financial Times reported вҒ on Monday.

item.title: Taco Bell, DunkinвҖҷ franchisee to pay $1.5 mln in NYC scheduling case
item.description: A Taco Bell and Dunkin franchisee has agreed to pay more than $1.5 million to settle claims by New York City that its managers at two dozen restaurants вҖӢviolated a local law requiring fast food businesses to give workers advance notice вҖҢof their schedules and other protections, Mayor Zohran MamdaniвҖҷs office said on Monday.

item.title: Microsoft-backed startup raises $40 million for advanced chipmaking equipment tech
item.description: Lace, a Norway-headquartered chipmaking equipment startup which is backed by Microsoft, has raised $40 million in funding to вҖҢfurther develop a technology that could enable significant advances in semiconductor design and manufacturing, the company said on Monday.

item.title: CERAWEEK US, TotalEnergies to shift nearly $1 billion from wind to oil and gas
item.description: The United вҖӢStates and French energy major TotalEnergies said on Monday they вҖҢwould redirect nearly $1 billion from offshore wind leases to U.S. oil and natural gas production.

item.title: UK considers expanding regulatory powers to tackle energy price gouging, profiteering
item.description: Britain's Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on вҖӢMonday that his government вҖҢmay consider expanding the powers of the competition regulator to вҖӢtackle price gouging вҖӢand profiteering in the energy вҒ market, given the impact вҖӢof the Middle East conflict.

item.title: CERAWEEK US needs more energy development to power AI, Google president says
item.description: The U.S. may вҖӢnot be scaling вҖҢelectricity fast enough for the expansion вҖӢof artificial вҖӢintelligence, Google's President and вҒ Chief Investment вҖӢOfficer Ruth Porat вҖӢsaid on Monday, speaking about the vast вҖӢamounts of вҖӢpower needed to scale вҖҢthe вҒ company's AI data centers.

item.title: Exclusive: US SEC's ex-enforcement chief clashed with bosses before leaving, sources say
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's top enforcement official, who left abruptly last week, had clashed with agency leaders over the direction of its enforcement program, including the handling of cases with ties to President вҖӢDonald Trump and his family, according to three people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Chevron wants to see more changes to Venezuela hydrocarbons law
item.description: Chevron вҖӢis seeing progress in Venezuela as its oil production in the OPEC country rises, but more вҖҢwork is needed to change key energy legislation and secure conditions for investment, CEO Mike Wirth said on Monday.

item.title: US construction spending unexpectedly falls in January
item.description: U.S. construction spending unexpectedly fell in January amid broad weakness in private projects, government data showed.

item.title: F1 owner Liberty Media can ride out Mideast conflict
item.description: The Middle East conflict has cut Formula OneвҖҷs race calendar and battered the share price of its owner Liberty Media , but market analysts believe вҖӢboth will ride out the crisis with long-term prospects intact.

item.title: Alphabet's Wing to start drone delivery in San Francisco Bay Area
item.description: Alphabet-owned Wing plans to begin delivering packages by drone to homes in California's San Francisco Bay Area in вҖӢthe coming months, it said on Monday, extending its вҖҢrollout to one of its earliest testing grounds.

item.title: CERAWEEK Oil prices have not climbed enough to cause demand destruction, US Energy Secretary Chris Wright says
item.description: Global oil prices have вҖӢnot climbed enough вҖҢto cause demand destruction, U.S. Energy Secretary вҖӢChris Wright вҖӢsaid on Monday at вҒ the CERAWeek energy вҖӢconference in Houston вҖӢTexas.

item.title: Fed's Miran still believes Fed should cut interest rates - Bloomberg TV
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor вҖӢStephen Miran said on Monday itвҖҷs premature to draw conclusions about how surging oil prices will affect the U.S. economy, as вҖҢhe stuck to his guns and argued a softening jobs market requires more rate cuts from the central bank.

item.title: HSBC appoints David Rice as first chief AI officer
item.description: HSBC has appointed David Rice вҖӢas its first ever вҖҢChief AI Officer, the British bank said вҖӢon Monday, as вҖӢit seeks to cut costs вҒ by increasing the вҖӢuse of generative AI вҖӢtechnology across its businesses worldwide.

item.title: Fed's Goolsbee: Inflation now the greater risk, watching expectations closely - CNBC
item.description: Inflation is the greater risk facing the U.S. вҖӢeconomy right now with the вҖҢunemployment rate remaining fairly stable, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee said on вҖӢMonday, though a quick вҖӢresolution of the Iran conflict could вҒ still leave the Fed poised вҖӢto cut rates later this вҖӢyear.

item.title: DoorDash rolls out emergency relief program as surging pump prices hit gig workers
item.description: Food delivery company DoorDash said on вҖӢMonday it is launching an вҖҢemergency relief program to help U.S. gig workers offset rising fuel costs.

item.title: Alibaba launches latest agentic AI platform with international unit's Accio Work
item.description: Alibaba has вҖӢpushed further into the global race for agentic artificial intelligence, with its вҖҢinternational commerce division launching Accio Work, a plugвҖ‘andвҖ‘play вҖңAI taskforceвҖқ it says can autonomously run complex business operations for small and medium-sized enterprises.

item.title: Nebius says "well-funded" for AI race after closing $4.3 billion debt raise
item.description: Nebius вҖӢclosed a $4.34 billion convertible debt funding round on Monday, with a вҖҢcompany executive saying that the European AI infrastructure firm is now "well-funded" to meet its 2026 capital spending plans of $16 billion to 20 billion.
